selection_policy: Four diverse reviewed section groups selected to stress-test the qualitative commentary schema only. No full claim extraction, production JSON normalization, database export, or final research dataset was created.
source_truth_note: Original PDFs remain source truth. Markdown sections are working text only. Tables/charts/numeric values require separate PDF QA before research use.

samples.coverage_type: prose-heavy sector narrative plus policy/programme material
samples.why_selected: Contains agriculture-sector narrative, named policy/regulatory initiatives, multiple schemes, actors, constraints, and cause/effect-style wording. Good stress test for actor, programme, policy_action, reform, constraint, and numeric sensitivity fields.

Almost entirely dense indicator tables with footnote/status markers and noisy Markdown table structure. Good stress test for evidence_type, source_table_or_figure, indicator_or_subject, data_status_flags, numeric_or_table_sensitive, and requires_pdf_numeric_qa.

Includes Digital Pakistan Policy, e-commerce regulatory framework, and BISP disbursement material with named institutions, implementation status, planned approvals, and beneficiary/payment references. Good stress test for policy_or_programmes arrays, actors, commitment_or_plan, programme_or_project, and numeric QA flags.

Combines broken table layout, broken words, and explicit encoding artifacts from the Markdown layer. Good stress test for markdown_quality_flags and extraction guidance around low-confidence text without treating table/numeric values as source truth.
